Operation 'create-bank-transactions' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The description lacks clarity on what the operation returns (push operation object with operation key) and important side effects (asynchronous processing, monitoring capability). Added details about the response structure and how to track the operation status, which are critical for AI agents to understand the operation's full lifecycle and how to use the returned operation key.

data.current_description:
The *Create bank transactions* endpoint creates new [bank transactions](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/BankTransactions) for a given company's connection.

[Bank transactions](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/BankTransactions) are records of monetary amounts that have moved in and out of an SMB's bank account.

**Integration-specific behaviour**

The required properties may vary based on the integration. For detailed requirements specific to each accounting software, refer to the API reference examples.
Alternatively, you can view the [Get create bank transaction model](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/operations/get-create-bank-transactions-model) for more information.
data.description_suggestion: Creates new bank transactions for a specified company connection and bank account in the target accounting software. Bank transactions are records of monetary amounts that have moved in and out of an SMB's bank account. The endpoint accepts a batch of transactions and returns a push operation object containing the operation key and status. Required properties vary by accounting software integration - use the Get create bank transactions model endpoint to determine integration-specific requirements. Transactions are asynchronously processed and can be monitored via the push operation key.
data.reasoning: The description lacks clarity on what the operation returns (push operation object with operation key) and important side effects (asynchronous processing, monitoring capability). Added details about the response structure and how to track the operation status, which are critical for AI agents to understand the operation's full lifecycle and how to use the returned operation key.

message: Operation 'get-create-bank-transactions-model' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary 'Get create bank transactions model' is vague and doesn't convey the actionable purpose. Description lacks clarity on what the response contains (field requirements, data types, validation rules) and why an AI agent would use this endpoint (to determine requirements before creating transactions). Improved summary is more action-oriented and the description explains the practical use case and response content.

data.current_description:
The *Get create bank account transactions model* endpoint returns the expected data for the request payload when creating [bank account transactions](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/BankTransactions) for a given company and integration.

[Bank account transactions](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/BankTransactions) are records of money that has moved in and out of an SMB's bank account.
 
**Integration-specific behaviour**

See the *response examples* for integration-specific indicative models.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve required fields for bank transaction creation
data.description_suggestion: Returns the expected request payload schema for creating bank transactions for a specified company and accounting software integration. This endpoint provides integration-specific field requirements, data types, and validation rules needed to successfully create bank transactions. Use this endpoint before calling Create bank transactions to understand which fields are required or optional for the target accounting software. The response includes indicative models demonstrating the expected structure for different integrations.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Get create bank transactions model' is vague and doesn't convey the actionable purpose. Description lacks clarity on what the response contains (field requirements, data types, validation rules) and why an AI agent would use this endpoint (to determine requirements before creating transactions). Improved summary is more action-oriented and the description explains the practical use case and response content.

message: Operation 'get-create-operation' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Description lacks clarity on what the response contains (operation status, timestamps, error details) and the practical use case (monitoring progress of submitted requests). Added details about response structure and operation states to help AI agents understand what information they'll receive and how to interpret it for monitoring purposes.

data.current_description:
The **Get create operation** endpoint returns a specific [write operation](/using-the-api/push) identified by the `pushOperationKey` that was performed on the company.

Write operations are actions that send requests to Codat, enabling the creation, updating, deletion of records, or uploading attachments in the connected accounting software.

For bank feeds, your push operations will only relate to the `bankTransactions` data type.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the status and details of a specific write operation identified by the pushOperationKey. Write operations are actions that send requests to Codat for creating, updating, or deleting records in the connected accounting software. For bank feeds, push operations relate exclusively to the bankTransactions data type. The response includes the operation status (pending, success, or failed), timestamps, and any error details if the operation failed. Use this endpoint to monitor the progress of previously submitted bank transaction creation requests.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity on what the response contains (operation status, timestamps, error details) and the practical use case (monitoring progress of submitted requests). Added details about response structure and operation states to help AI agents understand what information they'll receive and how to interpret it for monitoring purposes.

message: Operation 'list-create-operations' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_input_details. Description lacks details about response structure (pagination, operation details, status values) and available filtering/pagination parameters. Missing context about practical use cases (auditing, monitoring multiple operations). Added information about response format, parameter support, and use cases to help AI agents understand how to query and interpret results effectively.

data.current_description:
The **List create operations** endpoint returns a list of [write operations](/using-the-api/push) performed on the company.

Write operations are actions that send requests to Codat, enabling the creation, updating, deletion of records, or uploading attachments in the connected accounting software. 

For bank feeds, use this endpoint to view write operations related to the `bankTransactions` data type.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of write operations performed on a company. Write operations are actions that send requests to Codat for creating, updating, or deleting records in the connected accounting software. For bank feeds, this endpoint returns write operations exclusively related to the bankTransactions data type. The response includes operation details such as status (pending, success, or failed), timestamps, data types, and error information. Supports filtering and pagination parameters to narrow results. Use this endpoint to audit all bank transaction operations or monitor the status of multiple submitted transactions.
data.reasoning: Description lacks details about response structure (pagination, operation details, status values) and available filtering/pagination parameters. Missing context about practical use cases (auditing, monitoring multiple operations). Added information about response format, parameter support, and use cases to help AI agents understand how to query and interpret results effectively.

message: Operation 'list-connections' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details. Original summary and description are too generic. Added context about pagination, what connections represent, and use case guidance for AI agents to understand when to call this operation.

data.current_description: List the connections for a company.
data.summary_suggestion: List all connections for a company
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all data connections established for a specified company. Returns a paginated list of connection objects, each representing a link between the company and a supported accounting software platform. Use this to view the status and details of all active and inactive connections for a company.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are too generic. Added context about pagination, what connections represent, and use case guidance for AI agents to understand when to call this operation.

message: Operation 'create-connection' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Description lacks clarity about what the operation returns and the broader context of the authorization flow. Added details about return value, what platformKey represents, and the connection establishment process for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
Creates a connection for the company by providing a valid `platformKey`. 

Use the [List Integrations](https://docs.codat.io/platform-api#/operations/list-integrations) endpoint to access valid platform keys.
data.description_suggestion: Establishes a new data connection between a company and a supported accounting software platform by providing a valid `platformKey`. The platformKey identifies the target accounting platform (e.g., Xero, QuickBooks). Returns the newly created connection object with its unique connectionId. Use the List Integrations endpoint to retrieve available platformKeys. This operation initiates the authorization flow with the target platform.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity about what the operation returns and the broader context of the authorization flow. Added details about return value, what platformKey represents, and the connection establishment process for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'delete-connection' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Added clarity about the permanent nature of the operation, what happens to previously synced data, and the expected response. This helps AI agents understand the full implications and consequences of calling this operation.

data.current_description:
Revoke and remove a connection from a company.
This operation is not reversible. The end user would need to reauthorize a new data connection if you wish to view new data for this company.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently revokes and removes a data connection from a company. This operation is irreversible and will terminate the authorization with the target accounting platform. Previously synced data remains in Codat but no new data will be pulled. The company must create a new connection and reauthorize if they wish to resume data sharing. Returns a success response with no content on completion.
data.reasoning: Added clarity about the permanent nature of the operation, what happens to previously synced data, and the expected response. This helps AI agents understand the full implications and consequences of calling this operation.

message: Operation 'unlink-connection' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary was too vague ('Unlink' is unclear). Description needed clarification about the return value and distinction from deletion. Enhanced to help AI agents understand when to use unlink versus delete.

data.current_description: This allows you to deauthorize a connection, without deleting it from Codat. This means you can still view any data that has previously been pulled into Codat, and also lets you re-authorize in future if your customer wishes to resume sharing their data.
data.description_suggestion: Deauthorizes a data connection while preserving it in Codat, allowing the company to re-authorize later. Unlike deletion, this operation maintains access to all previously synced data and enables future reauthorization without losing historical records. Returns the updated connection object with deauthorized status. Use this when a company temporarily wants to stop data sharing but may resume later.
data.reasoning: Summary was too vague ('Unlink' is unclear). Description needed clarification about the return value and distinction from deletion. Enhanced to help AI agents understand when to use unlink versus delete.

message: Operation 'get-configuration' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Original summary and description lack domain context. Added specificity about what 'configuration' means in the bank feeds context (sync settings, mapping rules). Clarified the return value and use case for AI agents.

data.current_description: The *Get configuration* endpoint returns the current configuration for a given company ID.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current bank feeds configuration for a company, including settings that control how bank transactions are synchronized and mapped to the target accounting software. Returns a Configuration object containing the company's sync preferences and mapping rules. Use this to view the existing configuration before making updates.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description lack domain context. Added specificity about what 'configuration' means in the bank feeds context (sync settings, mapping rules). Clarified the return value and use case for AI agents.

message: Operation 'set-configuration' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original summary and description are extremely vague and lack domain context. Added specificity about what configuration controls (sync preferences, mapping rules), what inputs are expected, what is returned, and the operational effect. Critical for AI agent understanding of this operation's purpose.

data.current_description: Use *Set configuration* endpoint to configure a given company ID.
data.description_suggestion: Configures bank feeds settings for a company, including sync preferences and transaction mapping rules for the target accounting software. Accepts a Configuration object specifying the desired settings. Returns the updated Configuration object reflecting the new settings. This operation enables or modifies how bank transactions are synchronized and mapped for the company's connections.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are extremely vague and lack domain context. Added specificity about what configuration controls (sync preferences, mapping rules), what inputs are expected, what is returned, and the operational effect. Critical for AI agent understanding of this operation's purpose.

data.current_description: Use the _Get last successful sync_ endpoint to obtain the status information for the company's [most recent successful sync](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/CompanySyncStatus).
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve last successful bank feed sync status
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the status information for the company's most recent successful bank feed sync. Returns a CompanySyncStatus object containing sync metadata, timestamp, and completion details. Useful for monitoring the latest successful data synchronization to the accounting software.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description lacks clarity about what status information is returned and the practical use case. Enhanced summary specifies 'bank feed' context and added output details about the response structure for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'get-create-bankAccounts-model' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary is awkward and unclear about purpose. data.current_description:
The *Get create/update bank account model* endpoint returns the expected data for the request payload when creating and updating a [bank account](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/BankAccount) for a given company and integration.

[Bank accounts](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/BankAccount) are financial accounts maintained by a bank or other financial institution. 

**Integration-specific behaviour**

See the *response examples* for integration-specific indicative models.
data.summary_suggestion: Get bank account schema for integration
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the expected request payload schema for creating or updating a bank account for a specific company and accounting software integration. data.current_description:
The _Batch create source accounts_ endpoint allows you to create multiple representations of your SMB's bank accounts within Codat's domain. The company can then map the source account to an existing or new target account in their accounting software.

> ### Versioning
> If you are integrating the Bank Feeds solution with Codat after August 1, 2024, please use the v2 version of the API, as detailed in the schema below. For integrations completed before August 1, 2024, select the v1 version from the schema dropdown below.
data.description_suggestion: Creates multiple source account representations for an SMB's bank accounts within Codat's domain. Returns a batch response (201 for full success, 207 for partial success) containing created source account objects. Source accounts can then be mapped to target accounts in the accounting software. Supports both v1 and v2 API versions depending on integration date.
data.reasoning: Original summary lacks specificity about 'batch' operation. Description is missing output details (response codes and structure). Enhanced summary clarifies batch operation. Improved description explains the response codes (201/207), what is returned, and the next step in the workflow, making it clearer for AI agents to understand success conditions and outcomes.

data.current_description:
The _Create Source Account_ endpoint allows you to create a representation of a bank account within Codat's domain. The company can then map the source account to an existing or new target account in their accounting software.
 
> ### Versioning
> If you are integrating the Bank Feeds solution with Codat after August 1, 2024, please use the v2 version of the API, as detailed in the schema below. For integrations completed before August 1, 2024, select the v1 version from the schema dropdown below.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a source account representation for a bank account within Codat's domain. Returns a SourceAccount object (v1) or SourceAccountV2 object (v2) containing the created account details. The source account can then be mapped to an existing or new target account in the accounting software. Supports both v1 and v2 API versions depending on integration date.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and appropriately distinguishes from batch operation. Description lacks output details about what is returned. message: Operation 'list-companies' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Description is overly verbose with repetitive company definition. Missing clarity on pagination support (5 parameters suggest pagination). Needs to explicitly state what is returned (paginated collection) and consolidate filtering information for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
The *List companies* endpoint returns a list of [companies](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/Company) associated to your instances.

A [company](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/Company) represents a business sharing access to their data.
Each company can have multiple [connections](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/Connection) to different data sources, such as one connection to Xero for accounting data, two connections to Plaid for two bank accounts, and a connection to Zettle for POS data.

## Filter by tags

The *List companies* endpoint supports the filtering of companies using [tags](https://docs.codat.io/using-the-api/managing-companies#add-metadata-to-a-company). It supports the following operators with [Codat's query language](https://docs.codat.io/using-the-api/querying):
 
- equals (`=`)
- not equals (`!=`)
- contains (`~`)

For example, you can use the querying to filter companies tagged with a specific foreign key, region, or owning team: 
- Foreign key: `uid = {yourCustomerId}`
- Region: `region != uk`
- Owning team and region: `region = uk && owningTeam = invoice-finance`
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of all companies associated with your instance. Supports filtering by tags using query operators (equals, not equals, contains) and Codat's query language. Returns a paginated collection of company objects, each representing a business with access to their data and multiple potential connections to data sources.
data.reasoning: Description is overly verbose with repetitive company definition. Missing clarity on pagination support (5 parameters suggest pagination). Needs to explicitly state what is returned (paginated collection) and consolidate filtering information for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
The *Delete company* endpoint permanently deletes a [company](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/Company), its [connections](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/Connection) and any cached data. This operation is irreversible.

A [company](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/Company) represents a business sharing access to their data.
Each company can have multiple [connections](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/Connection) to different data sources, such as one connection to Xero for accounting data, two connections to Plaid for two bank accounts, and a connection to Zettle for POS data.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a company, all its associated connections, and any cached data. This operation is irreversible and cannot be undone. Requires the company ID as input. Returns a success response with no content on successful deletion.
data.reasoning: Summary is good. Description clearly states the destructive nature and side effects, but lacks explicit information about what response is returned (likely 204 No Content or similar). Adding response details improves AI agent understanding of operation completion.

data.current_description:
Use the *Replace company* endpoint to replace the existing name, description, and tags of the company. Calling the endpoint will replace existing values even if new values haven't been defined in the payload.

 A [company](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/Company) represents a business sharing access to their data.
Each company can have multiple [connections](https://docs.codat.io/bank-feeds-api#/schemas/Connection) to different data sources, such as one connection to Xero for accounting data, two connections to Plaid for two bank accounts, and a connection to Zettle for POS data.
data.description_suggestion: Replaces the company's name, description, and tags with the values provided in the request body. This is a full replacement operation - any fields not included in the payload will be cleared or set to null. Requires the company ID and a request body with the new company details. Returns the updated company object.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity on the full replacement behavior (fields not in payload are cleared). Missing explicit information about required inputs (companyId parameter and request body structure) and what is returned. Improved version clarifies the PUT semantics for AI agents.

message: Operation 'get-company-access-token' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_context. Description explains purpose and use cases well, but lacks explicit details about what is returned (token object structure) and clarifies token validity period (one day = 24 hours). data.current_description:
Use the _Get company access token_ endpoint to return an access token for the specified company ID. The token is valid for one day. 

The token is required by Codat's embeddable UIs (such as [Connections SDK](https://docs.codat.io/auth-flow/optimize/connection-management) and [Link SDK](https://docs.codat.io/auth-flow/authorize-embedded-link)) to verify the identity of the user and improve the reliability of data provided by them.
data.description_suggestion: Generates a short-lived access token for the specified company that is valid for 24 hours. This token is required by Codat's embeddable UIs (Connections SDK and Link SDK) to authenticate the user and ensure data reliability. Returns an access token object containing the token string and expiration details.
data.reasoning: Description explains purpose and use cases well, but lacks explicit details about what is returned (token object structure) and clarifies token validity period (one day = 24 hours). Missing information about response format helps AI agents understand how to use the returned token.

data.current_description:
The _Update source account_ endpoint updates a single source account for a single data connection connected to a single company.

### Tips and pitfalls

* This endpoint makes it possible to update the `accountName`, `status`, and `balance` fields.
    * The `status` field can only be updated to 'disconnected'.
    * The `balance` field can only be updated on accounts that are **not** 'connected'.
* Updates made here apply exclusively to source accounts and will not affect target accounts in the accounting software.
data.description_suggestion: Updates a source account's properties for a specific company and connection. Supports updating `accountName`, `status`, and `balance` fields with the following constraints: the `status` field can only be updated to 'disconnected', and the `balance` field can only be updated on accounts that are not 'connected'. Returns the updated source account object. Updates apply only to source accounts and do not affect target accounts in the accounting software.
data.reasoning: The current description contains useful constraint information but lacks clarity about what is returned and doesn't explicitly state that this is a partial update operation. The suggestion consolidates the tips into the main description for better AI agent interpretability and adds explicit mention of the return value.

data.current_description:
The _Delete source account_ endpoint enables you to remove a source account.

Removing a source account will also remove any mapping between the source bank feed bank accounts and the target bankfeed bank account.
data.description_suggestion: Removes a source account from a company's connection. Deleting a source account automatically removes any existing mappings between the source bank account and target bank accounts in the accounting software. This action cannot be undone.
data.reasoning: The current description mentions the side effect of removing mappings but could be clearer about the permanence of the action and the scope of what gets deleted. The suggestion improves clarity and adds important context about irreversibility for AI agents.

message: Operation 'generate-credentials' has semantic quality issues: inconsistent_terminology, missing_output_details. The description uses 'revoke credentials endpoint' inconsistently with the actual operation name 'delete-bank-feed-credentials'. The suggestion clarifies the terminology, explicitly states what is returned, and makes the QuickBooks Online limitation more prominent for AI agent routing.

data.current_description:
The _Generate bank account credentials_ endpoint can be used to generate credentials for QuickBooks Online to authenticate the Bank Feed in the QBO portal. Each time this endpoint is called, a new set of credentials will be generated.

The old credentials will still be valid until the revoke credentials endpoint is used, which will revoke all credentials associated to the data connection.

> **For QuickBooks Online only**
>
> Only call this endpoint when onboarding SMBs that use  QuickBooks Online.
data.description_suggestion: Generates authentication credentials for QuickBooks Online bank feed setup. Each call produces a new credential set while previous credentials remain valid until revoked. Returns the generated credentials for use in the QBO portal. Previous credentials can be revoked using the delete credentials endpoint. **Note: QuickBooks Online only.**
data.reasoning: The description uses 'revoke credentials endpoint' inconsistently with the actual operation name 'delete-bank-feed-credentials'. The suggestion clarifies the terminology, explicitly states what is returned, and makes the QuickBooks Online limitation more prominent for AI agent routing.

message: Operation 'delete-bank-feed-credentials' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. The current description is overly formal and uses 'revoke' and 'delete' inconsistently. data.current_description:
The _Delete Bank Account Credentials_ endpoint serves as a comprehensive mechanism for revoking all existing authorization credentials that a company employs to establish its Bank Feed connection.

In cases where multiple credential sets have been generated, a single API call to this endpoint revokes all of them.
data.description_suggestion: Revokes all authentication credentials associated with a company's bank feed connection. If multiple credential sets have been generated, this operation invalidates all of them simultaneously. After deletion, previously generated credentials will no longer be valid for bank feed authentication.
data.reasoning: The current description is overly formal and uses 'revoke' and 'delete' inconsistently. The suggestion uses clearer, more direct language, explicitly states the effect on previously generated credentials, and clarifies that this is an irreversible action affecting all credentials.

The summary says 'List bank feed accounts' but the operation actually lists mappings, not just accounts. data.current_description:
The *List bank accounts* endpoint returns information about a source bank account and any current or potential target mapping accounts.
 
A bank feed account mapping is a specified link between the source account (provided by the Codat user) and the target account (the end user's account in the underlying software).

> **For custom builds only**
> 
> Only use this endpoint if you are building your own account management UI.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves bank feed account mappings for a company's connection, including source account details and available target account options. A bank feed account mapping links a source account (provided by the Codat user) to a target account in the accounting software (end user's account). Use this endpoint to discover valid mapping options before creating new mappings. **For custom account management UI builds only.**
data.reasoning: The summary says 'List bank feed accounts' but the operation actually lists mappings, not just accounts. The description uses inconsistent terminology ('List bank accounts' vs 'bank feed account mapping'). The suggestion clarifies the purpose, explains the relationship between source and target accounts, and indicates this is a discovery endpoint for mapping options.

| Xero                  | Yes       |
| Exact                 | No        |
| FreeAgent             | No        |
| Sage                  | No        |
